content.full_text:
March 26, 1986


Rozellah Ryan, Staff Counsel
Department of Corrections
630 K Street
Sacramento, CA  95814

                                          Re: FPPC Advice No. A-86-066

Dear Ms. Ryan:

     Thank you for your memorandum of February 24, 1986, which
confirmed our telephone discussion of February 20, 1986,
regarding gift reporting pursuant to Government Code Section
87207.

     Your memorandum correctly states my advice that when an
official receives a gift valued at $50 or more, the gift must
be reported on the official's Statement of Economic Interests,
even if several donors provided the gift and each of them paid
less than $50.  However, in such a situation, the official is
not required to disclose the name of each donor, but may refer
to the donors as a group, such as, "A group of my friends and
co-workers, each of whom paid less than $50."

     If an official receives a gift from more than one donor and
some or all of the donors have paid $50 or more toward the gift,
the official may refer to the donors as a group, but must also
provide in an attachment the name of each donor who paid $50 or
more and the amount paid by the donor.  (See FPPC regulation
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18726.6, copy enclosed.)

     I hope this additional information is helpful. Please call
me at (916) 322-5662 if I can be of further assistance.

                                          Sincerely,

                                          [signature]
                                          Carla Wardlow
                                          Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure




428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

State of California

Memorandum

Date : February 24, 1986

To : CARLA WARDLOW
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street, P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

From : Department of Corrections, Sacramento 95814

Subject: GOVERNMENT CODE §87207

The purpose of this memo is to confirm in writing our telephone discussion on
February 20. During that discussion I asked for the FPPC interpretation of the
reporting requirement in Government Code §87207. The statute requires that the
source of gifts of $50 or more be reported. I asked whether the individual
donor must give a cumulative gift of $50 or whether all donors, including those
giving less than $50 gifts, must be reported if the official has received a
total of $50 in gifts during the reporting period.

It is my understanding that you have stated that FPPC requires that only those
donors who have given gifts of $50 need be reported. The exception is if the
numerous donors, whose individual gifts are under $50, are part of a group so
as to be considered a single donor for purposes of FPPC. In such a case, all
donors would need to be reported.

Thank you for your assistance.

ROZELLAH RYAN
Staff Counsel

GA-47-B

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 26, 1986


Rozellah Ryan
Staff Counsel
Department of Corrections
630 K Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: A-86-066

Dear Ms. Ryan:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act has been received by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice
request, you may contact the Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division at (916) 322-5662.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or unless more information is needed to answer your request,
you should expect a response within 21 working days.

                                    Very truly yours,

                                    [signature]

                                    Jeanne Pritchard
                                    Chief
                                    Technical Assistance and Analysis
                                    Division

sections.question: When an official receives a gift valued at $50 or more from multiple donors where each individual donor paid less than $50, must all individual donors be reported on the Statement of Economic Interests? Additionally, when some or all donors have paid $50 or more toward a gift, what disclosure is required?
sections.conclusion: When an official receives a gift valued at $50 or more, the gift must be reported on the Statement of Economic Interests even if several donors provided the gift and each paid less than $50. In such a situation, the official is not required to disclose the name of each donor, but may refer to donors as a group. If some or all donors have paid $50 or more toward the gift, the official may refer to donors as a group but must also provide in an attachment the name of each donor who paid $50 or more and the amount paid by that donor.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asked whether the $50 gift reporting threshold applies to individual donors or cumulative gifts from multiple donors under Government Code Section 87207, and what disclosure requirements apply when multiple donors contribute to a single gift.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Individual donors are only reportable if they personally contributed $50 or more. Multiple donors contributing less than $50 each can be referred to as a group without individual identification, but if any donor contributed $50 or more, those specific donors must be named with amounts.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains three parts: acknowledgment letter (Feb 26), memorandum confirming discussion (Feb 24), and formal advice letter (Mar 26). The formal advice letter from Carla Wardlow is the primary FPPC response. Clear question and conclusion sections present in the memorandum and formal advice letter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: When an official receives a gift valued at $50 or more from multiple donors where each individual donor paid less than $50, must all individual donors be reported on the Statement of Economic Interests? Additionally, when some or all donors have paid $50 or more toward a gift, what disclosure is required?

CONCLUSION: When an official receives a gift valued at $50 or more, the gift must be reported on the Statement of Economic Interests even if several donors provided the gift and each paid less than $50. In such a situation, the official is not required to disclose the name of each donor, but may refer to donors as a group. If some or all donors have paid $50 or more toward the gift, the official may refer to donors as a group but must also provide in an attachment the name of each donor who paid $50 or more and the amount paid by that donor.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice clarifying gift reporting requirements under Government Code Section 87207, specifically addressing whether the $50 threshold applies to individual donors or aggregate gifts and what disclosure is required for group gifts.
